name: Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act
owner: US Department of Health and Human Services
description: US federal law (Pub. L. 104-191, 1996) establishing data protection requirements for healthcare information. Privacy Rule (45 CFR 160, 164) regulates use and disclosure of Protected Health Information (PHI); Security Rule (45 CFR 164.302-318) requires administrative, physical, and technical safeguards for ePHI; Breach Notification Rule (45 CFR 164.400-414) requires reporting of unauthorized PHI disclosures.
relationToCloudSecurity: Cloud service providers handling PHI (e.g., as Business Associates) must comply with the Security Rule. Common cloud-vendor compliance overlay; most major CSPs offer HIPAA-compliant service configurations and sign BAAs with covered entities.
relationToAISecurity: AI systems processing health data (clinical decision support, medical imaging analysis, etc.) must comply with HIPAA when handling PHI. Recent HHS guidance addresses AI-specific privacy considerations.

license.notes: US federal statutes are public-domain government works (17 USC §105) and may be freely redistributed.

lifecycle.notes: Privacy Rule effective 2003, Security Rule effective 2005, Omnibus Rule (HITECH) 2013. Continuously enforced by HHS OCR.
